title: /k
summary_text: `/k` is not a documented chat command in this repo. The repo does document `/kernel` as the Kernel-design scope in [AGENTS.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/AGENTS.md), and the local launcher behavior is defined in [scripts/local/launchers/kernel](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/launchers/kernel) and [scripts/local/kernel-entrypoint.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/kernel-entrypoint.sh). On this machine, `k` is installed and currently points at this active run: `adhoc:MacBook-Pro-M5-5:kernel-workspace:42297:run:20260402T093918:54970` Bounded status: - state: `running`, lifecycle `blocked` - blocking reason: `bootstrap-receipt` - runtime health: `invalid` - reason: `codex-provider-evidence-missing` Useful local commands: - `k open` to attach/recover the current run - `k show <run_id>` to inspect it - `k new <purpose>` to start a new one If you meant “switch to Kernel work” rather than inspect the launcher, state the Kernel task and I’ll work in that scope.
